edges.description: Deregulated cilium-dependent Hedgehog/GLI signaling disrupts dorsoventral patterning and midline development of the brain, producing corpus callosum agenesis and cerebellar vermis hypoplasia.
edges.source: KIF7 Cilium-Tip Dysfunction and Impaired Hedgehog Signal Transduction

edges.description: Deregulated Hedgehog/GLI3 output also disrupts anterior-posterior limb patterning and midline craniofacial development.
edges.source: KIF7 Cilium-Tip Dysfunction and Impaired Hedgehog Signal Transduction

edges.description: Abnormal callosal development produces the eponymous corpus callosum agenesis phenotype.
Abnormal cerebellar development produces vermis hypoplasia or dysgenesis.
Severe cerebellar and brainstem malformation can produce the molar tooth sign.
The CNS malformation burden contributes to neurodevelopmental impairment and intellectual disability.
Abnormal limb patterning produces postaxial polydactyly.
Abnormal limb patterning produces preaxial polydactyly.
Preaxial foot patterning disruption produces hallux duplication.
Digital patterning disruption produces soft-tissue syndactyly.
Midline craniofacial patterning disruption produces cleft palate.
ACLS craniofacial patterning disruption contributes to macrocephaly.
Craniofacial patterning disruption produces a prominent forehead.

nodes.description: Agenesis or hypoplasia of the corpus callosum is one of the two cardinal features of ACLS and gives the syndrome its name.

evidence.reference_title: Novel KIF7 Mutation in a Tunisian Boy with Acrocallosal Syndrome: Case Report and Review of the Literature.
evidence.snippet: Acrocallosal syndrome (ACLS) is a rare autosomal recessive disorder characterized by agenesis of the corpus callosum

nodes.description: Cerebellar vermis dysgenesis/hypoplasia, sometimes severe enough to produce the molar tooth sign, occurs in ACLS and overlaps with Joubert syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Novel KIF7 mutations extend the phenotypic spectrum of acrocallosal syndrome.
evidence.snippet: vermis dysgenesis with brainstem anomalies (molar tooth sign), strongly indicated the diagnosis

nodes.description: Impaired cilium-dependent Hedgehog signaling during neurodevelopment produces the hallmark CNS malformations of ACLS: agenesis or hypoplasia of the corpus callosum and cerebellar vermis dysgenesis. In a subset of patients the cerebellar/brainstem malformation is severe enough to generate the molar tooth sign on MRI, overlapping with Joubert syndrome and reflecting the shared ciliary Hedgehog mechanism.

evidence.reference_title: Novel KIF7 mutations extend the phenotypic spectrum of acrocallosal syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Acrocallosal syndrome (ACLS) is a rare recessive disorder characterised by corpus callosum agenesis or hypoplasia, craniofacial dysmorphism, duplication of the hallux, postaxial polydactyly, and severe mental retardation

evidence.reference_title: Novel KIF7 mutations extend the phenotypic spectrum of acrocallosal syndrome.
evidence.snippet: vermis dysgenesis with brainstem anomalies (molar tooth sign), strongly indicated the diagnosis

nodes.description: Cleft palate occurs in a subset of ACLS cases and is also a feature of the allelic hydrolethalus syndrome, reflecting shared deregulation of midline craniofacial Hedgehog patterning.

evidence.reference_title: KIF7 mutations cause fetal hydrolethalus and acrocallosal syndromes.
evidence.snippet: polydactyly, brain abnormalities and cleft palate

nodes.description: Cutaneous (soft-tissue) syndactyly of the digits is a recognized digital malformation in ACLS, co-occurring with polydactyly.

evidence.reference_title: Molar tooth sign and acrocallosal syndrome--a report on a Polish family and review of KIF7 syndromology.
evidence.snippet: characterized by postaxial and/or preaxial polydactyly, cutaneous syndactyly, macrocephaly, widely spaced eyes, absence or hypoplasia of the corpus callosum, and intellectual disability

nodes.description: Duplication of the hallux (great toe) is a distinctive preaxial digital malformation in ACLS.

evidence.reference_title: Novel KIF7 mutations extend the phenotypic spectrum of acrocallosal syndrome.
evidence.snippet: corpus callosum agenesis or hypoplasia, craniofacial dysmorphism, duplication of the hallux, postaxial polydactyly

nodes.description: Widely spaced eyes (hypertelorism) are part of the characteristic craniofacial dysmorphism of ACLS.

evidence.reference_title: Novel KIF7 mutations extend the phenotypic spectrum of acrocallosal syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Facial dysmorphism with hypertelorism and prominent forehead in all the cases

nodes.description: Intellectual disability, frequently severe, is a core neurodevelopmental feature of ACLS.

evidence.reference_title: Molar tooth sign and acrocallosal syndrome--a report on a Polish family and review of KIF7 syndromology.
evidence.snippet: absence or hypoplasia of the corpus callosum, and intellectual disability

nodes.description: KIF7, the human ortholog of Drosophila Costal2, is an immotile kinesin-4 protein that localizes to the tip of the primary cilium, where it organizes the cilium-tip compartment and regulates the GLI transcription factors of the Hedgehog pathway. In the absence of Hedgehog ligand KIF7 promotes processing of GLI3 into its transcriptional repressor form; upon pathway activation KIF7 and GLI accumulate at the cilium tip. Biallelic loss-of-function KIF7 mutations deregulate most GLI target genes and impair GLI3 processing, disrupting the tightly dosed Hedgehog output required for midline brain, limb, and craniofacial patterning.
nodes.id: KIF7 Cilium-Tip Dysfunction and Impaired Hedgehog Signal Transduction

evidence.reference_title: KIF7 mutations cause fetal hydrolethalus and acrocallosal syndromes.
evidence.snippet: KIF7, the human ortholog of Drosophila Costal2, is a key component of the Hedgehog signaling pathway

evidence.reference_title: KIF7 mutations cause fetal hydrolethalus and acrocallosal syndromes.
evidence.snippet: we show deregulation of most GLI transcription factor targets and impaired GLI3 processing in tissues from individuals with KIF7 mutations

evidence.reference_title: KIF7 mutations cause fetal hydrolethalus and acrocallosal syndromes.
evidence.snippet: Our data show the role of KIF7 in human primary cilia, especially in the Hedgehog pathway through the regulation of GLI targets, and expand the clinical spectrum of ciliopathies

evidence.reference_title: Hedgehog-induced ciliary trafficking of kinesin-4 motor KIF7 requires intraflagellar transport but not KIF7's microtubule binding.
evidence.snippet: In vertebrates, Hedgehog signaling requires the primary cilium, and KIF7 and Gli transcription factors accumulate at the cilium tip in response to Hedgehog activation

evidence.reference_title: Hedgehog-induced ciliary trafficking of kinesin-4 motor KIF7 requires intraflagellar transport but not KIF7's microtubule binding.
evidence.snippet: we show that the immotile behavior of KIF7 is required to prevent ciliary localization of Gli transcription factors in the absence of Hedgehog signaling

nodes.description: KIF7-dependent Hedgehog/GLI3 signaling is required for dosed limb and craniofacial patterning. Deregulated GLI output produces polydactyly, hallux duplication, syndactyly, cleft palate, and the characteristic macrocephalic craniofacial appearance.

evidence.reference_title: KIF7 mutations cause fetal hydrolethalus and acrocallosal syndromes.
evidence.snippet: overlapping features that include polydactyly, brain abnormalities and cleft palate

evidence.reference_title: Molar tooth sign and acrocallosal syndrome--a report on a Polish family and review of KIF7 syndromology.
evidence.snippet: characterized by postaxial and/or preaxial polydactyly, cutaneous syndactyly, macrocephaly, widely spaced eyes, absence or hypoplasia of the corpus callosum, and intellectual disability

nodes.description: Macrocephaly (enlarged head) is a consistent craniofacial feature of ACLS.

evidence.reference_title: Molar tooth sign and acrocallosal syndrome--a report on a Polish family and review of KIF7 syndromology.
evidence.snippet: characterized by postaxial and/or preaxial polydactyly, cutaneous syndactyly, macrocephaly, widely spaced eyes, absence or hypoplasia of the corpus callosum, and intellectual disability

nodes.description: The molar tooth sign on MRI (deepened interpeduncular fossa with elongated, thickened superior cerebellar peduncles) can occur in ACLS, reflecting the shared ciliary Hedgehog mechanism with Joubert syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Molar tooth sign and acrocallosal syndrome--a report on a Polish family and review of KIF7 syndromology.
evidence.snippet: They present with features like molar tooth sign and hyperventilation that are not very typical in ACLS, but do occur in other ciliopathies

nodes.description: Postaxial polydactyly of the hands is one of the two cardinal limb features of ACLS, reflecting deregulated Hedgehog/GLI3 anterior-posterior limb patterning.

evidence.reference_title: Molar tooth sign and acrocallosal syndrome--a report on a Polish family and review of KIF7 syndromology.
evidence.snippet: Acrocallosal syndrome is a multiple congenital anomaly disorder characterized by postaxial and/or preaxial polydactyly

nodes.description: Preaxial polydactyly of the feet, frequently manifesting as duplication of the hallux, is characteristic of ACLS.

evidence.reference_title: Novel KIF7 Mutation in a Tunisian Boy with Acrocallosal Syndrome: Case Report and Review of the Literature.
evidence.snippet: postaxial polydactyly of the hands as well as preaxial polydactyly of the feet

nodes.description: A prominent (bossed) forehead is a consistent craniofacial feature of ACLS.

evidence.reference_title: Novel KIF7 mutations extend the phenotypic spectrum of acrocallosal syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Facial dysmorphism with hypertelorism and prominent forehead in all the cases
